machinery_genes.quote: the integral inner membrane proteins comprising Yts1C, Yts1M, Yts1K, and Yts1F
machinery_genes.note: Iwobi et al. 2003, the founding paper that identified and characterized the Yts1 T2SS gene cluster (yts1C-S) in high-pathogenicity Y. enterocolitica 1B; the same cluster is present in the 8081 genome (the paper examined the 8081 genome sequence and the secreton is shared across biotype 1B). GspC-family role assigned from the apparatus brief; the paper groups Yts1C with the integral inner-membrane proteins.

machinery_genes.quote: the two outer member proteins or secretins, Yts1D and Yts1S
machinery_genes.note: Iwobi et al. 2003. Yts1D is the main GspD-family secretin; the paper notes yts1D shares the highest identity (60-80%) with homologous secretons of other species.

machinery_genes.quote: the cytoplasmic, energy-transducing Yts1E protein (which interacts with protein L)
machinery_genes.note: Iwobi et al. 2003. GspE-family secretion ATPase; named with its interaction partner (protein L = Yts1L).

machinery_genes.quote: the integral inner membrane proteins comprising Yts1C, Yts1M, Yts1K, and Yts1F
machinery_genes.note: Iwobi et al. 2003. GspF-family integral IM component.

machinery_genes.quote: the four type 4 pilin-like proteins (pseudopilins), Yts1G, Yts1H, Yts1I, and Yts1J
machinery_genes.note: Iwobi et al. 2003. Yts1G is the major (GspG-family) pseudopilin; the paper notes yts1G is among the genes sharing highest identity (60-80%) with homologous secretons.

machinery_genes.quote: the four type 4 pilin-like proteins (pseudopilins), Yts1G, Yts1H, Yts1I, and Yts1J
machinery_genes.note: Iwobi et al. 2003. GspH-family minor pseudopilin.

machinery_genes.quote: the four type 4 pilin-like proteins (pseudopilins), Yts1G, Yts1H, Yts1I, and Yts1J
machinery_genes.note: Iwobi et al. 2003. GspI-family minor pseudopilin.

machinery_genes.quote: the four type 4 pilin-like proteins (pseudopilins), Yts1G, Yts1H, Yts1I, and Yts1J
machinery_genes.note: Iwobi et al. 2003. GspJ-family minor pseudopilin.

machinery_genes.quote: the integral inner membrane proteins comprising Yts1C, Yts1M, Yts1K, and Yts1F
machinery_genes.note: Iwobi et al. 2003 groups Yts1K with the integral inner-membrane proteins; by operon position (yts1C-S) and the GspK nomenclature it is the GspK-family minor pseudopilin/platform component.

machinery_genes.role: inner-membrane platform component (binds the secretion ATPase)
machinery_genes.quote: the cytoplasmic, energy-transducing Yts1E protein (which interacts with protein L)
machinery_genes.note: Iwobi et al. 2003. 'Protein L' = Yts1L, the GspL-family IM platform component that interacts with the Yts1E ATPase (mirrors the EpsE-EpsL interaction in V. cholerae).

machinery_genes.quote: the integral inner membrane proteins comprising Yts1C, Yts1M, Yts1K, and Yts1F
machinery_genes.note: Iwobi et al. 2003. GspM-family integral IM platform component.

machinery_genes.quote: the Yts1O protein, a putative prepilin-like peptidase
machinery_genes.note: Iwobi et al. 2003. GspO-family prepilin peptidase that processes the Yts1 pseudopilins.

machinery_genes.quote: the two outer member proteins or secretins, Yts1D and Yts1S
machinery_genes.note: Iwobi et al. 2003. Yts1S is the terminal gene of the yts1C-S cluster; the paper groups it with the outer-membrane/secretin proteins. GspS-family (PulS-like pilotin/accessory) per the apparatus brief nomenclature.

notes: Yts1 = the virulence-associated T2SS unique to high-pathogenicity (biotype 1B) Y. enterocolitica, first identified and characterized by Iwobi et al. 2003 (Infect Immun) using representational difference analysis between the nonpathogenic 1A strain NF-O and its 1B counterpart WA-314. That paper also examined the near-complete genome of Y. enterocolitica 8081 (this instance's strain) and reported the same yts1 cluster. The apparatus is the yts1C-S gene cluster, named after the standard Gsp/type II nomenclature. The Discussion explicitly assigns each gene a role: integral IM platform = Yts1C, Yts1M, Yts1K, Yts1F; cytoplasmic ATPase = Yts1E (interacts with protein L = Yts1L); pseudopilins = Yts1G, Yts1H, Yts1I, Yts1J; secretins/OM proteins = Yts1D and Yts1S; prepilin peptidase = Yts1O. This yields 13 machinery genes (C, D, E, F, G, H, I, J, K, L, M, O, S). The paper explicitly states the cluster LACKS a yts1N gene ('The putative yts1 cluster described here lacks a yts1N gene, in contrast to its counterpart in V. cholerae'), so no Yts1N is listed despite the operon label 'yts1A-N'. No yts1A or yts1B genes are named in the literature for the apparatus; the label range in the corpus appears nominal, and only C-S are characterized. Lower-confidence calls: Yts1K (grouped with IM proteins by the paper rather than explicitly as a pseudopilin; family assigned from GspK position) and Yts1S (grouped with secretins/OM proteins; GspS pilotin-family role inferred from operon position and brief nomenclature). Deliberately excluded as non-machinery: the secreted substrates ChiY, EngY/YE2830, and YE3650 (the effectors of this instance, characterized by Shutinoski et al. 2009); the PclR transcriptional regulator (regulator, not apparatus); and the Sec/Tat translocon. All gene-naming quotes are verbatim from the Iwobi et al. 2003 PMC full text (PMC152056). DOI and PMID confirmed real via get_article_metadata.
